title: Little One — Experience Schema v1
description: The capability-bounded declarative language that the fixed engine (assets/js/player.js) renders. Generated content produces INSTANCES that conform to these experience TYPES; adding a new TYPE (a new branch here) is a class change and is human-gated. SAFETY MODEL: a closed field vocabulary + additionalProperties:false everywhere means the language literally cannot express a network call, storage write, or script — no such field exists — so generated UI is sandboxed by what the schema can SAY, not by trusting a reviewer to catch a bad line of code. This file is the contract the Gardener generates against and the gate validates (schema/validate.py, the Guardian's mechanical arm). Art-key EXISTENCE and content-safety (framing/PII) are enforced separately by .github/gardener/lint.rb.

artKey.description: A picture key. Must exist in assets/js/art.js — existence is enforced by lint.rb (the Guardian's registry check), not by this schema.

storyPage.description: One page of a story. Exactly one of three TYPES: a plain tap-along page, a lift-the-flap page, or the on-device camera-mirror page.

story.description: A tap-along story: pages the grown-up reads aloud while the child taps the picture.

evidence.description: relative path under raw/research/ backing a developmental claim

game.description: TYPE 'find' — a forgiving Tap & Find game: 'can you find X?' among picture choices; the right tap cheers, a wrong tap nudges. No fail state.

mode.description: optional; 'find' is the default game type

ask.description: optional prompt template with a {label} slot, e.g. 'Who says {label}?'

oddOneOutGame.description: TYPE 'odd-one-out' — 'which one is different?': the engine shows a majority group + one outlier; tapping the odd one cheers, a wrong tap nudges. Critical thinking; no fail state.

groups.description: categories the round is built from — a majority group + one outlier

anyGame.description: A word-game is exactly one game TYPE. Adding a new type here is a class change (human-gated).
